title: Create migration 044: Create hr_employee_current_snapshot table
description: Create hr_employee_current_snapshot table to store pre-computed current employee state per NIK using ROW_NUMBER() ranking

files.change: Create hr_employee_current_snapshot table with NIK partitioning

implementation: Create table hr_employee_current_snapshot with columns: id, nik, current_emp_code, current_emp_name, current_loc_code, current_hr_status, active_count, total_row_count, is_ambiguous, created_at, updated_at
Create UNIQUE INDEX IX_snapshot_nik ON hr_employee_current_snapshot(nik)
Insert initial data from db_ptrj.HR_EMPLOYEE using ROW_NUMBER() ranking
Rank by: Status='1' first, UpdateDate DESC, CreateDate DESC, EmpCode DESC
Only insert rows where current_rank = 1 (active/current employee)

reference.pattern: Use ROW_NUMBER() OVER PARTITION BY pattern from PRD
reference.examples: HR Resolution Rule: ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY LTRIM(RTRIM(NewICNo)) ORDER BY CASE WHEN Status='1' THEN 0 ELSE 1 END, UpdateDate DESC, CreateDate DESC, EmpCode DESC)

criteria: Initial data loaded with NIK ranking
is_ambiguous flag set for NIKs with multiple rows

rationale.chosen_approach: Snapshot table for performance - compute ranking once, query many times
alternatives_considered: On-the-fly ranking - rejected due to performance overhead on every sync
decision_factors: Data freshness acceptable (daily refresh is sufficient)
rationale.tradeoffs: Requires periodic refresh but query performance is optimal

manual_checks: Verify NIK 1906041207910002 maps to current_emp_code A0966
success_metrics: No NULL nik values in snapshot
